name: mami

relevance.justification: Contains species/genus-level microbiome data across 6 maternal and 4 neonatal body sites (QIIME2/Silva + MetaPhlAn3), is structured and actively maintained. Fails the full demographic requirement: BMI and ethnicity are absent; disease/phenotype data missing — only delivery mode, gestational age, sex, and feeding regimen are captured per sample. Score breakdown: +2 species-level microbiome, +1 structured, +1 actively maintained, +1 partial identifiers = 5/9.

novelty.justification: No existing BioThings API covers mother-infant microbiome with multi-site body-site sampling (6 maternal + 4 neonatal sites) across 39,000+ samples from 29 countries. Entity types (maternal/neonatal microbiome samples, probiotic strains) and relations (vertical transmission, site-specific microbiome composition) are entirely absent from MyGene, MyDisease, MyChem, or MyVariant. Additionally includes a probiotic strain knowledge base linking MicrobeWiki, GenBank, AEProbio, and Google Patents.

openness.justification: Database access stated as 'freely accessible to all academic users' — the academic-only qualifier creates a usage restriction. No explicit open license (CC0, CC-BY, etc.) stated for the database content itself. The paper is published under CC-BY-NC 4.0, which governs the article text, not the underlying data. Homepage (https://bioinfo.biols.ac.cn/mami/) was DNS-unreachable at eval time, preventing verification of actual download terms. Two blockers: (1) no confirmed open data license, (2) academic-only access qualifier.

evidence.scope: Maternal and neonatal microbiome samples from 6 maternal body sites (gut, skin, nasal, oral, vagina, breast milk) and 4 neonatal sites (gut, skin, nasal, oral). Also includes a probiotic strain database. 39,000+ samples from 29 countries across 6 continents.
evidence.overlap: Raw reads sourced from EBI ENA, NCBI SRA, and MG-RAST (these are already publicly accessible). MAMI adds re-processed harmonized microbiome profiles + metadata integration. No direct overlap with any BioThings API data source.

license.name: CC BY-NC 4.0 (paper only; database license unstated)
restrictions: CC-BY-NC applies to the published article, not confirmed for database content
Database described as 'freely accessible to academic users' — commercial use restriction implied
No explicit open data license found for bulk data download

api_info.notes: No public REST/GraphQL API documented in the paper. Web interface provides search, browse, and export of specific sample subsets. No bulk API endpoint found.

risks: Homepage DNS-unreachable (bioinfo.biols.ac.cn) — server may be down or behind institutional firewall; long-term availability uncertain
No explicit open data license — 'academic users only' qualifier may preclude BioThings redistribution
BMI and ethnicity absent from metadata schema — limits utility for full demographic-microbiome KG
Probiotic database relies on scraped third-party sources (Wikipedia, Google Patents) with variable curation quality
next_actions: Verify homepage accessibility from non-restricted network before reconsidering
Contact MAMI maintainers (bioinfo.biols.ac.cn) to clarify data license and commercial redistribution policy
If license is confirmed open: reconsider as NEEDS_REVIEW (relevance 5 is below threshold but mother-infant microbiome is a gap)
Do NOT proceed to site inspection or plugin generation without license clarification
